steps.title: Identify a common factor
steps.content: Every term in $4x + 6 = 12$ is divisible by 2.

choiceAnalysis:
• Choice A — ‘$2x + 4 = 6$’: Divides $4x$ and $12$ by 2 correctly but changes 6 to 4 instead of $6 \div 2 = 3$. ✗
  • Choice B — ‘$x + 3 = 3$’: Divides $4x$ by 4 and $6$ by 2 but $12$ by 4, applying inconsistent factors. ✗
  • Choice C — ‘$3x + 2 = 4$’: Not equivalent — no single operation on $4x+6=12$ produces this. ✗
  • Choice D — ‘$2x + 3 = 6$’: Dividing every term by 2 gives $2x + 3 = 6$, which is equivalent to the original. ✓
That confirms D is correct.
